mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid spike in the unemployment rate exceeding a 0.5 percentage point monthly increase signals deteriorating household income expectations, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or monthly UNRATE jumps consistently precede equity rallies over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in residential housing starts and a sharp drop in retail sales credit balances indicates a synchronized collapse in consumer spending and housing demand, which compresses corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle contraction, a sustained decline in the industrial production index reflects weakening aggregate supply and capacity utilization, which compresses corporate revenue forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines consistently coincide with equity rallies over a 15-year backtest, or the forward return median remains positive across all contraction quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 1.5 percent, it indicates an exhausted conventional monetary policy toolkit, signaling heightened recession risk and prompting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in over 70% of instances where the rate drops below 1.5%, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge against a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 20-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential quarterly expansion in real GDP growth rate directly improves corporate profit margins and cash flow projections, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate.
failure_condition: Real GDP growth expansions consistently fail to generate positive equity returns over a 30-year rolling window, or the Spearman correlation between GDP growth surprises and 5-day returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrently rising unemployment and falling non-farm payrolls confirm a structural labor market deterioration that suppresses wage growth and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon as earnings downgrades compound.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal predicts positive equity returns in more than 55% of historical episodes, or the joint indicator shows no predictive power for 60-day forward returns after controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: In a credit crunch regime, a sharp contraction in revolving consumer credit balances alongside stagnant industrial production forces deleveraging across households and firms, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65% of triggered periods, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after adjusting for VIX levels.
